title: Terminal Clearance Extrapolation — recovering a two-compartment PK decay law
strategies: constant baseline: predict the geometric-mean early concentration (ignores decay) -> ~0.1
mono-exponential C=a*exp(-k*t): least squares on log C, collapses distribution+elimination into one rate, wrong terminal slope on the tail
biexponential C=A*exp(-alpha t)+B*exp(-beta t) via rate grid-search with closed-form amplitude least squares, separating the slow elimination rate beta so it extrapolates the tail
alternatives: curve-peeling / residual stripping, nonlinear least squares refinement of the rates, weighting late draws, trading complexity penalty against terminal accuracy
